header.title: Stream Subtitle Translator
obs.overlay.instructions: Add this URL as an OBS Browser Source. Update the URL when VoiceSub bind address changes.
tools.runtime.note: Runtime logs and diagnostics are written to the user data folder.
translation.dispatcher.title: Translation dispatcher
translation.dispatcher.note: Increase timeout for slow providers. Higher parallelism uses more API quota.
translation.provider_limits.title: Per-provider dispatcher limits
translation.provider_limits.note: Leave empty to use provider defaults. Limits apply per provider name in the dispatcher.
tuning.source_lang.note: Hint for translation providers. Use auto to detect from recognition, or pick a fixed language code.
settings.webspeech.advanced.summary_hint: Worker lifecycle and partial filtering. Reopen the worker after changes.
settings.webspeech.advanced.hint: These options map to asr.browser and partial filters in the backend. Save config and restart recognition when needed.
settings.webspeech.advanced.partial.note: Filters tiny partial rewrites before they reach subtitles and translation.
worker.force_finalization_timeout_ms.not: How long to wait without partial updates before sending the current live text as a final segment.
command_palette.title: Command palette
config.save.applied_with_restart: Saved and applied immediately. {subject} changes will take effect {restartLabel}.
config.save.local_with_restart: Saved locally. {subject} changes will take effect {restartLabel}.
asr.worker_browser_next_open_log: [asr] Web Speech worker will use the selected browser on next open (desktop)
worker.open_external_failed_log: [browser-asr] failed to open external browser worker
worker.settings.default_status: This window prioritizes its own localStorage settings and mirrors them to backend config when possible.
worker.settings.saved_backend: Saved locally and mirrored to backend at {time}
worker.experimental.settings_local_only: Experimental worker settings are stored locally in the browser.
worker.visibility.hidden_warning: The Web Speech window is currently hidden or minimized. In this state, recognition can stall, end via onend, or stop producing partial/final results.
browser_asr.error.no_audio_track: The browser did not return an audio track.
browser_asr.error.open_mic_track: Could not open microphone audio track.
browser_asr.error.fallback_default_start: Could not fall back to the default recognition.start().
browser_asr.error.experimental_start_fai: Could not start experimental Web Speech recognition.
browser_asr.network.hint: Web Speech network error: recognition service unreachable (VPN, firewall, DNS, proxy, blockers). Check connectivity; changing the browser microphone usually does not fix this.
browser_asr.network.preflight_failed_log: Web Speech: network preflight failed — recognition cloud unreachable. Check VPN/firewall/DNS/proxy and press Start again.
browser_asr.error.phrases_retry: Web Speech: phrases-not-supported — retrying without on-device phrase hints.
browser_asr.error.language_retry: Web Speech: language-not-supported — one retry after clearing on-device hints.
overlay.preview.config_not_loaded: Subtitle style preview is unavailable until config loads.
overlay.preview.no_visible_lines: No visible subtitle lines for the current settings yet.
overlay.preset_hint.single: Single: all visible subtitle items are rendered inside one physical row in the saved order.
overlay.preset_hint.dual_line: Dual-line: the first visible item uses the top row, and the remaining visible items share the second row.
overlay.preset_hint.stacked: Stacked: each visible subtitle item gets its own row.
style.preset.default_description: Choose a preset and tweak it locally.
style.slot.enabled_hint: Selected slot: {slotLabel}. Empty values inherit from base.
style.slot.disabled_hint: Selected slot: {slotLabel}. Enable Override to reveal slot controls.
obs.cc.status.disabled: OBS Closed Captions are disabled. The browser overlay remains unchanged.
obs.cc.status.error: OBS captions are enabled but not connected: {error}
obs.cc.status.waiting: OBS captions are enabled and waiting for the OBS websocket connection.
language.label: Interface language
ui.layout.label: Dashboard layout
compact.recognition.hint: Recognition and microphone controls appear below while this tab is active.
settings.title: Settings
settings.fonts.hint: Subtitle font family, size, weight, and per-line overrides are on the Style tab.
settings.note: Provider API keys, profiles, import/export, and fonts remain in their dedicated sections. Use Save to persist layout and language.
runtime.progress.title: Runtime Progress
overview.transcript.title: Transcript
overview.preview.title: Subtitle Output Preview
overview.overlay.title: OBS Overlay URL
overview.recognition.title: Recognition
overview.recognition.worker_browser.web_: From this web dashboard, opening the worker uses your OS default browser. The worker browser choice applies only to the desktop app (pywebview launcher), which starts Google Chrome for the dedicated worker window.
overview.recognition.provider.hint: Backend providers are configured here for local microphone capture. Web Speech modes use the separate browser worker window.
overview.recognition.hint.browser_google: Keep the worker window visible while listening. Minimized or fully hidden windows are throttled by the browser and Web Speech may stall or drop audio. In the packaged desktop app, Browser Speech opens `/google-asr` (classic) or `/google-asr-experimental` (experimental) in Google Chrome with an isolated profile. Save, then open the worker again. Experimental mode is more sensitive when hidden.
overview.recognition.hint.browser_quick_: This session uses Web Speech. Change recognition settings in the overview cards, then Stop/Start if needed.
overview.recognition.hint.remote_worker_: Remote worker mode supports AI runtime only. Web Speech is disabled here.
overview.recognition.browser_mic_note: In Web Speech mode, switch microphone using the browser permission icon in the address bar.
overview.microphone.title: Microphone
save.status.default: Settings are written to disk when you press Save.
translation.title: Translation Settings
translation.cache.enable: Reuse translation cache (skip duplicate API calls)
translation.cache.persist: Save translation cache to disk between sessions
translation.lines.title: Translation Lines
translation.lines.note: Each slot can use its own language, provider, and label. Duplicate target languages are allowed.
translation.provider_settings.title: Provider Settings
translation.provider_settings.helper: Choose which provider's API key/model settings to edit. Each translation line can use its own provider.
translation.provider_settings.scope.sele: Editing shared settings for {provider}. This follows the selected line: {line}.
translation.provider_settings.scope.defa: Editing shared settings for {provider}. New lines default to {defaultProvider}.
translation.provider_settings.warning: Some enabled lines use providers that are not fully configured.
translation.line.title: Translation line {number}
translation.line.label: Line label
translation.latest.title: Translated Results
translation.result.disabled: Translation disabled or no translation lines configured.
translation.order.note: Translation is optional. Target language order is saved locally and reused by subtitle routing.
provider.google_translate_v2.hint: Primary recommended provider. Requires a Google Translate v2 API key.
provider.google_cloud_translation_v3.hin: Official Google Cloud Translation Advanced REST API. Requires project ID and OAuth access token, not a v2 API key.
provider.google_cloud_translation_v3.sta: Stable cloud translation provider. Uses the Advanced v3 endpoint.
provider.google_gas_url.hint: Experimental best-effort Google Apps Script bridge. Reliability depends on your script and deployment.
provider.google_gas_url.status: Experimental provider. Use it only if you control the GAS endpoint.
provider.google_web.hint: Experimental unofficial Google web endpoint. Best-effort only and may break anytime.
provider.google_web.status: Experimental provider. Not recommended as the primary path.
provider.azure_translator.hint: Stable provider. Requires Azure Translator API key and usually a region.
provider.deepl.hint: Requires a DeepL API key. API URL can usually stay at the default value.
provider.libretranslate.hint: Use your own LibreTranslate server or a managed endpoint. API key is optional depending on the server.
provider.libretranslate.status: Classic provider. Reliability depends on the endpoint you choose.
provider.openai.hint: Flexible LLM subtitle translation. Requires API key and model, and uses the built-in subtitle prompt unless overridden.
provider.openai.status: LLM provider. Quality depends on the selected model.
provider.openrouter.hint: OpenAI-compatible routing layer. Requires API key and model.
provider.openrouter.status: LLM provider. Quality depends on the selected model.
provider.lm_studio.hint: Local OpenAI-compatible server. Quality depends on the locally loaded model.
provider.lm_studio.status: Local provider. Quality depends on the selected local model.
provider.ollama.hint: Local OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Quality depends on the local model.
provider.ollama.status: Local provider. Quality depends on the selected local model.
provider.public_libretranslate_mirror.hi: Experimental public mirror. Availability can change without notice.
provider.public_libretranslate_mirror.st: Experimental public provider. Reliability is not guaranteed.
provider.free_web_translate.hint: Experimental no-key web provider. Best-effort only.
provider.free_web_translate.status: Experimental public provider. Behavior may change without notice.
subtitles.title: Subtitle Output
subtitles.timing.title: Subtitle Timing
subtitles.sync_expiry: Keep completed source visible while its translation is still visible
subtitles.allow_replace: Replace the visible block immediately when the next phrase finalizes
subtitles.timing.note.order: This order is used by both the dashboard preview and the OBS overlay payload. Visible translated lines are capped at 5.
subtitles.notes.title: Subtitle Routing Notes
subtitles.notes.preview: The live subtitle preview stays in the top overview area so you can always compare transcript, output timing, and device state without switching tabs.
subtitles.notes.routing: Use this section to control visibility, language order, overlay preset, and subtitle hold timing. The same saved routing state is shared by the dashboard preview and the OBS overlay.
style.title: Subtitle Style
style.description: Shared style state for dashboard preview and OBS overlay.
style.custom_name: Save current style as a custom preset
style.custom_status.default: Built-in presets are editable, and custom presets are saved locally in config/profiles.
style.fonts.title: Fonts
style.fonts.note: Project-local fonts are the reliable custom-font path. System font listing is best-effort.
style.fonts.status.default: Fallback fonts are always available. Local system font access depends on the browser/runtime.
style.ui_theme.title: UI Theme
style.ui_theme.note: Controls the dashboard + browser worker windows appearance (light/dark + gradient palette). Saved in config/profiles.
style.ui_theme.preview.title: Preview
style.ui_theme.status: Changes apply immediately. Use the global Save button to persist.
style.base.title: Base Style
style.base.note: Shared defaults applied to every visible subtitle line unless a line slot overrides them.
style.slots.title: Per-line Slot Overrides
style.slots.note: Use separate styling for the source line and up to five visible translated lines.
style.slots.description: Select a line slot to override the base style.
style.final_note: The same saved subtitle style payload drives this dashboard preview and the OBS overlay. Presets are a starting point, and your edits stay local in config/profiles.
obs.title: OBS Closed Captions
obs.enable: Send captions to OBS Closed Captions
obs.debug.enable: Mirror captions into an OBS text source for debugging
obs.debug.send_partials: Send partials to the debug text input
obs.source_live_partials: Send partial source updates in source_live mode
obs.min_partial_delta: Minimum text change before sending another partial
obs.final_delay: Delay before final replaces the previous text
obs.clear_after: Clear OBS text after this many milliseconds
obs.avoid_duplicates: Avoid sending identical caption text twice
obs.note.native: Native stream captions still use SendStreamCaption. The optional debug mirror writes the same text into a normal OBS text source via SetInputSettings, which is useful because native captions are not visible as a regular preview layer.
obs.state.title: OBS CC Status
obs.state.default: OBS Closed Captions are disabled by default.
obs.state.note: Recommended debug pattern: partials to the OBS text source mirror, finals to both the text source and native SendStreamCaption. first_visible_line follows your current Subtitle Output order and visibility.
tuning.quick.title: Recognition Feel
tuning.appearance.label: How quickly text appears
tuning.appearance.note: Higher values show in-progress text sooner. Lower values wait for a bit more speech.
tuning.finish.label: How quickly speech is considered finished
tuning.finish.note: Higher values finalize phrases faster after a pause. Lower values wait longer before closing the subtitle block.
tuning.stability.label: How steady or less chatty updates should be
tuning.stability.note: Higher values reduce tiny partial rewrites. Lower values feel more live but can flicker more.
tuning.rnnoise.note.1: 0% keeps the raw path. Higher values blend in more RNNoise output.
tuning.rnnoise.note.2: Optional and off by default. It can reduce false recognition from constant background noise, but it may also change recognition behavior.
tuning.rnnoise.note.3: Save config or profile after tuning.
tuning.preset.label: Latency preset
tuning.preset.note: Presets set streaming decode and word-growth partials. Moving sliders switches to Custom.
tuning.apply.full_note: After changing tuning values: click Save, then Stop/Start so recognition picks up the new settings.
tools.advanced.latency_preset.help: Applies a ready-made bundle of timing settings (Ultra low latency, Balanced, Quality). Choose Balanced unless you have a specific reason to chase speed or accuracy.
tools.advanced.streaming_decode.help: When enabled, recognition decodes only new audio since the last step. When disabled, each partial update re-runs on the entire phrase, which is heavier on long utterances.
tools.advanced.partial_emit_mode.help: Controls how partial (live) text grows on screen. word_growth adds words as they are recognized, similar to Web Speech. char_delta reacts to any character change and can flicker more.
tools.advanced.partial_min_new_words.hel: In word_growth mode, partial text updates only after at least this many new words appear. 1 gives the fastest typing effect; higher values reduce partial rewrites.
tools.runtime.title: Runtime Diagnostics
tools.runtime.logs_location: Logs are stored in logs/: backend.log, runtime-events.log, session-latest.jsonl.
tools.advanced.title: Advanced Realtime Tuning
tools.advanced.vad_mode.0: 0 - Fastest, most sensitive to speech and noise
tools.advanced.vad_mode.3: 3 - Strictest, least likely to trigger on noise
tools.advanced.vad_mode.help: Voice Activity Detection decides when microphone input counts as speech. Lower numbers react faster but may start on background noise; higher numbers wait for clearer speech. Mode 2 is a good balance for most rooms.
tools.advanced.partial_emit.help: Minimum time between partial subtitle updates in milliseconds. Lower values refresh text more often (faster feel) but may cause flicker; higher values are calmer.
tools.advanced.min_speech.help: How long you must speak before partial text appears. Very low values show text quickly but may trigger on brief noises; higher values wait for a clearer speech start.
tools.advanced.silence_hold: Keep phrase open through a short pause (ms)
tools.advanced.silence_hold.help: After speech pauses briefly, the engine keeps the current phrase open for this many milliseconds instead of finishing immediately. Helps short breath pauses inside one sentence.
tools.advanced.pause_finalize: Pause before the phrase is considered finished (ms)
tools.advanced.pause_finalize.help: How long silence must last before the spoken phrase becomes a finalized subtitle block. Shorter values finish phrases faster; longer values tolerate more pauses.
tools.advanced.max_phrase: Maximum phrase length before forced split (ms)
tools.advanced.max_phrase.help: Maximum continuous speech length before the engine forcibly splits the phrase. Prevents one very long utterance from staying as a single growing partial forever.
tools.advanced.min_delta.help: Partial text is updated only if it changed by at least this many characters. Higher values reduce small back-and-forth rewrites; lower values react to tiny edits.
tools.advanced.coalescing.help: Merges very rapid partial rewrites within this time window. Smooths out quick word corrections at the cost of slightly delayed updates.
tools.advanced.chunk_window.help: Advanced audio window size for backend chunking. Leave at 0 unless you are testing a specific backend compatibility scenario.
tools.advanced.chunk_overlap.help: Overlap between adjacent audio windows. Leave at 0 for normal use.
tools.advanced.ignore_quiet: Ignore very quiet input before ASR
tools.advanced.ignore_quiet.help: When enabled, very quiet input is discarded before recognition starts. Useful if background noise causes false triggers, but can miss soft speech if set too aggressively.
tools.advanced.min_rms: Ignore noise quieter than this level
tools.advanced.min_rms.help: Minimum loudness (RMS) required for audio to be considered. Raise only if quiet room noise keeps triggering recognition; too high a value can ignore real speech.
tools.advanced.min_voiced_ratio.help: Minimum fraction of audio frames that must look like speech. Helps filter non-speech noise; increase if false starts persist, decrease if soft speech is missed.
tools.advanced.first_partial: Speech needed before the first live text (ms)
tools.advanced.first_partial.help: How much speech is needed before the very first partial appears. Can differ from min speech if you want a cautious first display but faster updates afterward.
tools.advanced.note.local: These settings stay local. Save config or profile after changes; use Stop/Start to compare behavior safely.
tools.source_replacement.title: Word replacement (before translation)
tools.source_replacement.intro: Optional post-ASR layer: replaces listed words in recognized text before translation, subtitles, and OBS captions. It does not change how ASR itself works.
tools.source_replacement.builtin: Include built-in profanity list (English, Russian, Japanese, Korean, Chinese)
tools.source_replacement.list_empty: No custom entries yet. Add a word and a replacement above.
tools.source_replacement.custom_hint: Up to 100 custom pairs. Options above control case sensitivity and whole-word matching. Adding the same word again updates its replacement. Use the global Save button so the running backend applies changes.
tools.safe.title: Starting reference values
tools.defaults.title: Current project defaults
tools.defaults.intro: These are the default values now used by fresh project configs and installs:
tools.defaults.line3: Minimum text change before updating (chars): default `4`. Safer: `10+`.
tools.defaults.line4: Maximum phrase length before forced split (ms): default `5500`. Safer: `6000`.
tools.defaults.line5: Silence hold / pause to finalize (ms): default `180 / 350`. Safer: `160-220 / 350-450`.
tools.defaults.line6: Quiet-input gate: default `off`. Safer: still `off` unless noise causes false starts.
tools.safe.intro: These are practical ranges if you want fewer surprises while keeping the current non-streaming feel:
tools.safe.line1: Chunk window / overlap: safer `0 / 0` unless you are deliberately testing backend compatibility.
tools.safe.line4: Min RMS / Min voiced ratio: safer to leave disabled, or start around `0.001 / 0.25` only when noise is the real problem.
tools.events.title: Live Events
tools.config.title: Local Config
tools.profiles.title: Profiles
remote.tools.intro: Secondary LAN-only controller/worker tools. Keep this section closed unless you need pairing, worker runtime control, or bridge windows.
remote.tools.state.template: Remote state: enabled={enabled} role={role} session={session} active={active} controller_online={controller_online} worker_online={worker_online}
remote.tools.worker.sync_template: Worker settings synced: translation={translation} targets={targets} asr_mode={asr_mode}
help.title: Help & Program Guide
help.intro: This page explains the main dashboard, recognition modes, translation routing, subtitle output, OBS integration, profiles, diagnostics, and desktop/remote startup choices.
help.quick_start.title: Quick start checklist
help.quick_start.1: Choose the interface language in the top-right corner.
help.quick_start.2: Select a recognition method and microphone in the overview cards.
help.quick_start.3: Configure translation lines only if you need translated subtitles.
help.quick_start.4: Copy the OBS Overlay URL into an OBS Browser Source.
help.quick_start.5: Press Start. Use Stop/Start after changing the Web Speech recognition language.
help.recognition.title: Recognition modes
help.recognition.body: Web Speech opens a separate browser worker window and streams recognition results back to this app.
help.recognition.local: VoiceSub uses Web Speech in a dedicated worker window for browser-based recognition.
help.recognition.browser: Use Web Speech for a lightweight quick start or when browser-based recognition is enough.
help.recognition.mic: For Web Speech, switch microphones through the browser permission icon in the worker window address bar.
help.translation.title: Translation lines and providers
help.translation.body: Translation is optional. You can run source-only subtitles, translation-only subtitles, one translated line, or multiple translated lines. Each line has its own language, provider, label, and enabled state.
help.translation.providers: Provider settings are shared per provider, while each line chooses which provider to use.
help.translation.order: The saved language order controls the dashboard preview, overlay payload, and first-visible-line OBS caption mode.
help.translation.keys: API keys and local provider URLs stay in local config/profiles; choose no-key experimental providers only when best-effort quality is acceptable.
help.subtitles.title: Subtitle output behavior
help.subtitles.body: The Subtitles tab controls what is visible, how many translated lines can appear, which overlay preset is used, and how long completed source and translation blocks stay on screen.
help.subtitles.source_translation: Source text and translated text can be shown together or independently.
help.subtitles.ttl: Source and translation hold times are separate, so a late translation can remain visible even after the original text expires.
help.subtitles.replace: The early-replace option decides whether the next finalized phrase can replace the current completed block immediately.
help.style.title: Style presets and slot overrides
help.style.body: The Style tab controls the shared visual system used by the dashboard preview and OBS overlay. Start from a built-in preset, then adjust fonts, colors, outline, shadow, background, spacing, and animation effects.
help.style.presets: Custom presets are saved locally in config/profiles.
help.style.slots: Slot overrides let source and translation_1 through translation_5 have different styling.
help.style.fonts: Project-local fonts are exposed through the bundled font route and are the most reliable custom font path.
help.obs.title: OBS overlay and captions
help.obs.body: The overlay URL is the main OBS Browser Source output. OBS Closed Captions are optional and use OBS websocket when enabled.
help.obs.overlay: Use the overlay URL for visible subtitles in a browser source; it reconnects automatically when the websocket drops.
help.obs.cc: Use OBS Closed Captions when you want platform caption metadata in addition to the visible overlay.
help.obs.debug: The debug mirror can write the same caption text into a normal OBS text source for troubleshooting.
help.tools.title: Tools, data, profiles, and diagnostics
help.tools.body: Tools & Data contains runtime diagnostics, advanced recognition timing, optional post-ASR word replacement, live events, local config JSON, profile import/export, diagnostics export, and LAN remote utilities.
help.tools.config: Save Current Config writes the current dashboard state to local user data.
help.tools.profiles: Profiles are named saved sets for quickly switching stream setups.
help.tools.diagnostics: Export Diagnostics bundles useful local logs and runtime state for troubleshooting.
help.remote.title: Desktop launcher and LAN remote mode
help.remote.body: The normal app is local-first and localhost-only. Desktop startup profiles make the intended mode explicit: Web Speech quick start, NVIDIA GPU, CPU-only, Remote Controller, or Remote Worker.
help.remote.local: Use local profiles for single-machine streaming.
help.remote.controller: Remote Controller sends microphone audio to a LAN worker and receives transcript/translation events back.
help.remote.worker: Remote Worker runs local AI recognition for the controller and does not use Web Speech modes.
worker.title: Web Speech Window
worker.description: This worker captures microphone audio with the browser Web Speech API (SpeechRecognition) and forwards partial/final text to the local subtitle app over WebSocket.
worker.experimental.title: Web Speech Window (Experimental)
worker.experimental.description: This experimental worker opens one live microphone MediaStreamTrack and tries SpeechRecognition.start(audioTrack). If that is rejected, it can fall back to normal start().
worker.experimental.settings.note_body: Keeps one live microphone track open, rearms recognition with the same track when possible, and falls back to recognition.start() if start(audioTrack) is rejected.
worker.experimental.warning.body: Experimental mode is more sensitive to browser throttling. Keep this window open and visible while it is listening, or the audio-track path and quick rearms may become unstable.
worker.settings.title: Recognition Controls
worker.force_finalization: Force-complete the current live text if the browser never emits a final result
worker.force_finalization.note: Forced finalization uses a short idle timeout and sends the current live text to the main app as a final segment.
worker.status.title: Live Diagnostics
worker.status.label: Status
worker.microphone.note: Use the browser microphone/site-permission icon in the address bar to select and switch the microphone for this worker window.
worker.warning.title: Keep This Window Visible
worker.warning.body: Web Speech can become unstable if this window is minimized, fully hidden, or left in a throttled background state for too long. For the most reliable recognition, keep this window open and visible while it is listening.
worker.partial.title: Live Partial Text
worker.final.title: Last Final Text
overlay.debug.title: Overlay Debug
tools.tts.title: TTS module
tools.tts.description: Opens a separate window for subtitle speech synthesis and optional Twitch chat TTS.
tools.runtime.full_logging.hint: Compact logs are always written to logs/. Full logging adds verbose runtime events and JSONL traces (api, pipeline, ui, startup). Restart the app after saving.
subtitles.keep_completed_during_partial: Keep completed translations visible during an active source partial
When the source line is still growing as a partial, finished translation lines stay on screen instead of clearing early.
translation.latest.hidden: Translated results are hidden. Turn the toggle on to monitor live output.
worker.advanced.title: Advanced
style.preset.desc.clean_default: Neutral baseline: Inter on a transparent background with a minimal black outline.
style.preset.desc.streamer_bold: Loud display look: Oswald with a cyan fill and a hot-magenta glow for live gameplay.
style.preset.desc.dual_tone: Lato body with distinct fill colors per slot so source and each translation read at a glance.
style.preset.desc.compact_overlay: Source Sans 3 inside a tight semi-opaque black bar — small footprint, maximum legibility.
style.preset.desc.soft_shadow: Comfortaa with a wide diffused shadow and zero outline — feels airy, no edge crunch.
style.preset.desc.anime_stream: Mochiy Pop One for Latin/Japanese + Comfortaa Bold for Cyrillic — classic anime fansub caption.
style.preset.desc.accessibility_high_con: Pure white Montserrat Bold on a solid opaque black plate — WCAG AAA contrast in any environment.
style.preset.desc.dark_cinema: Playfair Display ivory on a solid warm sepia plate — letterboxed art-house aesthetic.
style.preset.desc.meeting_soft: Roboto Regular in light grey with no stroke and no plate — minimal, talking-head friendly.
style.preset.desc.retro_terminal: VT323 amber phosphor on a dark CRT panel — DEC VT320 / Apple II vibe with PT Mono for Cyrillic.
style.preset.desc.fallout_pipboy: Share Tech Mono in Pip-Boy phosphor green with a strong scanline glow.
style.preset.desc.comic_burst: Bangers in comic-yellow with a chunky black outline and a hot-red shadow — Marvel SFX panel energy.
style.preset.desc.cyberpunk_neon: Orbitron Black in hot magenta with a cyan halo glow on a deep navy plate.
style.preset.desc.noir_typewriter: Special Elite typewriter on a deep ink plate — 1940s detective / typewritten dossier mood.
style.preset.desc.vlog_pastel: Poppins on a warm pastel pill — cozy lifestyle / vlog look, plays nicely with soft backgrounds.
updates.banner.message: VoiceSub {latest} is available — you are on {current}.
